author: bharath.vedartham
content:
yeah i did consider that, the reject cases are:
```
- _[REJECT]_ `bid.builder_index` is a valid/active builder index -- i.e.
  `is_active_builder(state, bid.builder_index)` returns `True`. (1)
- _[REJECT]_ `bid.execution_payment == 0`. (2)
- _[REJECT]_ `bid.fee_recipient == proposer_preferences.fee_recipient`. (3)
- _[REJECT]_ The length of KZG commitments is less than or equal to the
  limitation defined in the consensus layer -- i.e. validate that
  `len(bid.blob_kzg_commitments) <= get_blob_parameters(compute_epoch_at_slot(bid.slot)).max_blobs_per_block`. (4)
- _[REJECT]_ The bid is for a higher slot than its parent block -- i.e. validate
  that `bid.slot` is greater than the slot of the block with root
  `bid.parent_block_root`. (5)
- _[REJECT]_ `bid.prev_randao` is the correct RANDAO mix -- i.e. validate that (6)
  `bid.prev_randao == get_randao_mix(parent_state, get_current_epoch(parent_state))`.
- _[REJECT]_ `signed_execution_payload_bid.signature` is valid with respect to
  the `bid.builder_index`. (7)
```
I dont think (1), (2), (4), (5), (7)  are possible.  I wonder if we are setting the fee recipient incorrectl

author: bharath.vedartham
content: but i assume the entire devnet has 1 fee recipient so its hard to get that wrong lmao

author: bharath.vedartham
content: unclear if the randao i m sending is wrong too. i m not sure if buildoor is doing anything wrong here. let me investigate more

author: nflaig
content: we aren't validating the randao yet on p2p though, that's a new gossip rule

author: bharath.vedartham

author: nflaig
content: yeah that's a funny thing I noticed before, so we publish the block first, then peers use getblobs and get them from EL then disseminate columns before we actually publish the payload, so in some cases we receive them via gossip before, so during publish it's tracked as duplicate and the publish count stays 0 hence the warning

author: pk910
content:
The lighthouse reveal issue is known too.
Lighthouse does not support the stateless reveal path with blob sidecars. It only accepts stateful submissions without blobs.
<@358120958726373381> is aware 😉

author: pk910
content:
the payload attributes are directly used for block building.  It contains information that need to go into the next payload,   like withdrawals and stuff like that.
Without the payload attributes, buildoor cannot ask the EL for a new payload, so it can't construct a bid either 🙂
Usually clients only emit the attributes when the next slot is proposed by a locally registered key, so the EL starts building a payload. Buildoor potentially builds payloads & bids for every slot,  even if no local proposer is available. Therefore most clients need that flag to emit attributes for all slots.  Not emitting payload attributes for all slot and also not providing a flag makes the client unusable for buildoor

author: bharath.vedartham
content: also most builders like flashbots, titan etc use the attributes. its very common practice for them to know the attributes on which the payload should be built on

author: bharath.vedartham
content: like withdrawals etc which would otherwise need to be derived from beacon state

author: pk910
content:
kurtosis config for glamsterdam-devnet-6 with builder deposit&exit tests + buildoor:
```yaml
participants:
  - el_type: ethrex
    el_image: ethpandaops/ethrex:glamsterdam-devnet-6
    cl_type: lodestar
    cl_image: ethpandaops/lodestar:glamsterdam-devnet-6
    count: 2
  - el_type: ethrex
    el_image: ethpandaops/ethrex:glamsterdam-devnet-6
    cl_type: teku
    cl_image: ethpandaops/teku:glamsterdam-devnet-6
    count: 2

network_params:
  gloas_fork_epoch: 1
  preset: mainnet
  seconds_per_slot: 6
  gas_limit: 150000000

mev_type: buildoor
additional_services:
  - dora
  - spamoor
  - assertoor

buildoor_params:
  image: ethpandaops/buildoor:glamsterdam-devnet-6
  builder_api: false
  epbs_builder: true
  extra_args:
    - --lifecycle
spamoor_params:
  image: ethpandaops/spamoor:master
  spammers:
    - scenario: eoatx
      config:
        throughput: 2
dora_params:
  image: ethpandaops/dora:glamsterdam-devnet-6
assertoor_params:
  image: ethpandaops/assertoor:glamsterdam-devnet-6
  run_stability_check: false
  run_block_proposal_check: false
  tests:
    - { file: "https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ethpandaops/assertoor/refs/heads/glamsterdam-devnet-6/playbooks/gloas-dev/builder-lifecycle.yaml", config: { builderCount: 2 } }
```

breaks after epoch 5 (builder exits seem to be the trigger).
but it's unfortunately not fully reliable. ethrex seems to have an issue too:  all ethrex nodes stay unpeered and tx propagation doesn't really work.
